name: mkdocs-juvix-plugin

metadata.name: mkdocs-juvix-plugin
metadata.summary: MkDocs documentation with support for Juvix Markdown files

documentation:
# Juvix MkDocs [![Mkdocs+Juvix CI](https://github.com/anoma/juvix-mkdocs/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/anoma/juvix-mkdocs/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![pages-build-deployment](https://github.com/anoma/juvix-mkdocs/actions/workflows/pages/pages-build-deployment/badge.svg)](https://github.com/anoma/juvix-mkdocs/actions/workflows/pages/pages-build-deployment)

This is a plugin for MkDocs to build static websites and highlight Juvix
codeblocks using [the Juvix Compiler](https://docs.juvix.org), and many more. It
also adds a CLI tool to help you get started with a new project called
`juvix-mkdocs`.

Checkout the [documentation website](https://anoma.github.io/juvix-mkdocs/) to
learn more.

modules.name: mkdocs_juvix.snippets
documentation:
Modified version of pymdownx.snippet extension to support custom Juvix/Isabelle
snippets by Jonathan Prieto-Cubides 2024.

Snippet ---8<---.

pymdownx.snippet Inject snippets

modules.name: mkdocs_juvix.env
documentation:
This file defines a configurator for the different plugins included in
mkdocs-juvix. It manages the different paths, mkdocs configurations, and
Juvix settings.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.env.ENV.get_filename_module_by_extension
documentation: The markdown filename is the same as the juvix file name but without the .juvix.md extension.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.env.ENV.find_file_in
documentation:
The filepath can be:
- Relative to the docs directory, e.g., "docs/..." or "./docs/..."
- Absolute, e.g., "/some/path/to/docs/..."
- Relative to the current working directory, in which case, relative_to
should be specified.

Otherwise, the search will be done relative to
self.CACHE_PROCESSED_MARKDOWN_PATH first, or relative to the docs
directory otherwise.

If the filepath is relative to the docs directory, the path to the
processed markdown file in the cache is obtained using
self.CACHE_PROCESSED_MARKDOWN_PATH. If the filepath is absolute, it is
checked for existence. If the filepath is relative to the current
working directory, relative_to is used to find the file.

functions.name: mkdocs_juvix.logger.get_plugin_logger
documentation:
Return a logger for plugins.

Arguments:
    name: The name to use with `logging.getLogger`.

Returns:
    A logger configured to work well in MkDocs,
        prefixing each message with the plugin package name.

Example:
    ```python
    from mkdocs.plugins import get_plugin_logger

    log = get_plugin_logger(__name__)
    log.info("My plugin message")
    ```

Initialize the logger adapter.

Arguments:
    prefix: The string to insert in front of every message.
    logger: The logger instance.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.logger.PrefixedLogger.process
documentation:
Process the message.

Arguments:
    msg: The message:
    kwargs: Remaining arguments.

Returns:
    The processed message.
A logger adapter to prefix log messages.

functions.name: mkdocs_juvix.common.preprocesors.links.process_wikilink

modules.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ
documentation:
This plugin provides a way to render diffs between files under the same
file name but with different version numbers in the same folder. The valid file
name pattern is `namevX.ext`, where `name` is the name of the file, `X` is the
version number, and `ext` is the file extension.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._match_versioned_file
documentation:
Checks if the given file path matches the pattern for a versioned file.
Pattern: `namevX.ext`, where `name` is the name of the file, `X` is the
version number, and `ext` is the file extension.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._get_name_version_number
documentation:
Extracts the name and version number from a file path. The filepath is
expected to be in the format `namevX.(extension)`, where `name` is the name
of the file and `X` is the version number.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._compute_filepath_version
documentation:
Computes the new filepath with an updated version number (integer) based on the original filepath.

Args:
    filepath (Path): The original filepath.
    counter (int): The counter to be added to the version number.

Returns:
    Optional[Path]: The new filepath with an updated version number, or None if the version is negative.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._run_diff
documentation:
Run the diff command between two files or directories. If
the files are in the same directory, the diff command is run
with the file names. If the files are in different directories,
the diff command is run with the absolute paths.

Args:
    _current (Path): The path to the current file or directory.
    _other (Path): The path to the other file or directory.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The diff output as a string. None if the diff command fails or the files do not exist or are directories.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._compute_diff_with_version
documentation:
Computes the difference between the given filepath and a different version
of the file.

Args:
    filepath (Path): The path to the file.

    counter (int): The counter indicating the version difference.
                A negative value indicates the previous version, while a
                positive value indicates the next version.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The difference between the two versions of the file as a
    string, or None if no difference is found.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._compute_diff_filename_version
documentation:
Computes the filename for a diff file with a modified version number.

Args:
    filepath (Path): The path to the original file.
    counter (int): The counter to add to the version number.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The computed filename for the diff file, or None if the version information is not available.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._write_diff_file_version
documentation:
Generate a diff file with a specific version number.

Args:
    filepath (Path): The path to the file.
    counter (int): The version number.

Returns:
    Optional[Path]: The path to the generated diff file, or None if no diff was generated.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.differ.DiffPreprocessor._write_diff_previous_next_version
documentation:
Writes the diff between the previous and next version of a file.
To compute the diff, the file name must match the pattern `namevX.ext`.

documentation: Support for wiki-style links in MkDocs in tandem of pydownx_snippets.

functions.name: mkdocs_juvix.links._get_alias_names
documentation: Returns the list of configured alias names.

documentation:
When MkDocs loads its files, extract aliases from any Markdown files
that were found.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.links.WikilinksPlugin.on_page_content
documentation:
Process the page content to add the wiki links to the page if the
frontmatter has the `list_wikilinks` flag set to true.

documentation: Auxiliary functions

classes.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.metadata
documentation:
Extract the metadata from the markdown output of the file.
Use it only after the markdown output is generated.
Metadata

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.is_cached
documentation:
Check if there is a cached markdown output generated by running `juvix
markdown`.
Caching

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.cached_hash
documentation:
Return the cached hash of the markdown output generated by running
`juvix markdown`. The hash is used to check if the file has changed.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.hash
documentation: Compute the hash of the file content of the original file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.changed_since_last_run
documentation:
Check if the original file has changed since the last time `juvix markdown`
was run on it, by checking if the hash of the file content at the original
location is different from the cached hash.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.markdown_output
documentation:
Return the cached output of running `juvix markdown` on the original file.
If the output is not cached, it runs `juvix markdown` on the original
file and returns the output.
Markdown Output property

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.run_pipeline
documentation:
Run the pipeline of tasks to generate the markdown output of the file.
Be aware that this may get the wrong output if the snippets in the file
are not cached.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.save_markdown_output
documentation:
Cache the input provided as the cached markdown. Update the hash of the
file to future checks.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.save_and_read_original_markdown_from_cache
documentation: Read the original markdown from the cache folder.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.has_error_message
documentation: Check if there is an error message for the given kind.
Error handling for markdown output

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.save_error_message
documentation: Save the error message to a cache file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.get_error_message
documentation: Retrieve the error message from cache or file of previous run.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.load_and_print_saved_error_messages
documentation: Print the error messages saved in the cache.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.clear_error_messages
documentation: Clear the error message from cache and file of previous run.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.add_errors_to_markdown
documentation:
Format the error message to include it in the Markdown output of the
file, so that it is rendered nicely. The filepath is used to extract the
metadata from the file if it is provided, otherwise the metadata is
extracted from the markdown output of the current file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._build_juvix_root_project_path_command
documentation: Root Juvix Project Path

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._build_juvix_markdown_command
documentation: Juvix Markdown tasks

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._run_command_juvix_markdown
documentation:
Run the Juvix Markdown command and return the output.
If the command fails, save the error message and return None.
The error message is saved to be displayed in the markdown output of
the file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._build_juvix_html_command
documentation: Juvix HTML tasks

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._process_juvix_html
documentation:
Generate the HTML output running the pipeline if the file has changed
since the last time `juvix html` was run on it. Otherwise, it reads
the cached HTML output from the cache file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._update_assets
documentation:
Update the assets in the cache folder. If the assets folder already exists
in the cache folder, it removes it. Then, it copies the assets folder from
the original folder to the cache folder.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._build_juvix_isabelle_command
documentation: Juvix to Isabelle translation tasks

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._run_juvix_isabelle
documentation:
Run the Juvix Isabelle command and return the output. If the command
fails, save the error message and return None. The error message is
saved to be displayed in the markdown output of the file. The output is
not Markdown, it is Isabelle/HOL code.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.process_isabelle_translation
documentation:
Process the Isabelle translation, saving the output to the cache folder.
If `modify_markdown_output` is True, the Isabelle output is added to the
`content` at the bottom.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._save_isabelle_theory
documentation:
Save the Isabelle output to the cache folder. This requires that the
Isabelle file name is known beforehand.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._fix_unclosed_snippet_annotations
documentation: TODO: Remove this once the compiler fixes this, positional comments
are preserved in the output

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile._skip_generation
documentation:
Skip the generation of the markdown output if the file has not changed
since the last time the pipeline was run on it.
Generate and save markdown output, the task run over either markdown or
Juvix Markdown files

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_original_markdown
documentation: Save the original markdown output for the file for later use.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_juvix_markdown
documentation: Generate the markdown output for the file.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_isabelle_theories
documentation: Process the Isabelle translation, saving the output to the cache folder.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_snippets
documentation:
Modify the markdown output by adding the snippets. This requires the
preprocess of Juvix and Isabelle to be ocurred before.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_wikilinks
documentation:
Modify the markdown output by adding the wikilinks. This requires the
preprocess of Juvix and Isabelle to be ocurred before.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.generate_images
documentation:
Modify the markdown output by adding the images. This requires the
preprocess of Juvix and Isabelle to be ocurred before.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownFile.write_errors_in_markdown
documentation:
Modify the markdown output by adding the errors. This requires the
preprocess of Juvix and Isabelle to be ocurred before.

A class that represents a Markdown file, which may contain:
- Juvix code blocks
- Wikilinks
- Images
- Incrusted Isabelle theories

classes.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.cache_orginals
documentation:
Cache the original Juvix Markdown files in the cache folder for faster
lookup.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.get_enhanced_file_entry
documentation:
Get the EnhancedMarkdownFile object for the given filepath in the docs
folder.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.hash
documentation:
Compute the hash of the folder containing the original Juvix Markdown
files. These files were moved to the cache folder during the build
process for faster lookup.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.has_changes
documentation:
Check if the markdown files, including Juvix Files, have changed since
the last time the project was built.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.update_cached_hash
documentation:
Update the cached hash of the entire project. Write it to the file
system for future lookups.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.is_html_cache_empty
documentation: Check if the folder containing the HTML cache is empty.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.save_juvix_modules_json
documentation:
Save the Juvix modules JSON file to the cache folder.
Return the path of the JSON file relative to the root of the project.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.run_pipeline_on_collection
documentation:
Process the files pipeline. First, generate the markdown output for all
the Juvix Markdown files. Then, generate the HTML output for all the
Juvix Markdown files.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.generate_html
documentation:
Generate the HTML output for all the Juvix Markdown files. In case the
`everything.md` file exists, we use it to generate the HTML output for
all the Juvix Markdown files. Otherwise, we generate the HTML output for
every Juvix Markdown file individually (not recommended).

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.clean_juvix_dependencies
documentation:
Clean the Juvix dependencies. This is necessary to avoid typechecking
errors when the Juvix compiler version changes.
Juvix dependencies

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.EnhancedMarkdownCollection.update_juvix_dependencies
documentation: Update the Juvix dependencies.
A collection of EnhancedMarkdownFile objects.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin.on_pre_build
documentation:
Aim to be fault-tolerant, so we don't care if some files fail
typechecking as part of the markdown processing, we include the error
message as part of the content of the page

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin.on_files
documentation:
List of the files to be included in the final build. These are copied to
the site directory.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin.on_page_markdown
documentation:
For Juvix Markdown files, we remove the `.juvix` suffix from the file
name. This is done to avoid having to change the file name in the
navigation menu and to make the URLs consistent.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin.move_html_cache_to_site_dir
documentation:
Move the HTML cache to the site directory. Otherwise, the HTML files
pointing to the e.g., Standard Library files will not accessible.
Notice that as part of the build process, we need to remove/rename the
HTML files generated for the Juvix Markdown files. These files are not
the right ones but the output by MkDocs.

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin.on_serve
documentation: Serve (Not important for the build process but for the development process)

methods.name: mkdocs_juvix.main.JuvixPlugin._generate_code_block_footer_css_file
documentation: CSS file generation
